title: Observed variables versus latent causes
learningObjectives: Explain observed variables versus latent causes in plain language
Write down the objective and identify its assumptions
Design one test that could falsify an over-strong interpretation

whyThisLesson.text: We observe activations and labels; latent causes are the hidden variables proposed to explain why those observations covary. This lesson gives you a concrete test for that claim before the course moves to the next abstraction.

intuition.analogy: Think of hearing several instruments through a wall. The sound at the wall is observable; the instruments are the proposed hidden causes. We observe activations and labels; latent causes are the hidden variables proposed to explain why those observations covary.
intuition.analogyLimit: Latent recovery claims are conditional: remove an assumption and the same observations can support a different latent explanation.
intuition.text: We observe activations and labels; latent causes are the hidden variables proposed to explain why those observations covary.

toyExample.description: Mix two non-Gaussian sources, then change the mixing matrix. Ask which assumptions make unmixing possible and which leave several answers equally valid.

steps.result: Specify exactly what observed variables versus latent causes should recover, preserve, or ignore.
steps.insight: The intended factor must be known before a toy result can be judged.

steps.result: Use the same data, capacity, training budget, and evaluation examples for both methods.
steps.insight: Otherwise a visible gain may be a resource difference in disguise.

steps.result: Vary the source correlation, noise, view quality, or sparsity and record the first failure.
steps.insight: The boundary of success is usually more useful than the headline score.

visual.question: Which assumption behind observed variables versus latent causes changes the result most?
visual.description: Move one control at a time. Treat the display as a toy model, then write down the real experiment it suggests.

technical.text: We observe activations and labels; latent causes are the hidden variables proposed to explain why those observations covary. In practice, write down the input unit, the operation applied to it, and the exact statistic used to score the result. Those are different things. A learned representation can be useful even when the interpretation attached to it turns out to be wrong.

mathematics.explanation: For this lesson, the expression makes the central trade-off explicit. We observe activations and labels; latent causes are the hidden variables proposed to explain why those observations covary.
mathematics.whyNeeded: It says precisely what the method is being rewarded for.
mathematics.ifRemoved: You could not tell whether two implementations were solving the same problem.
mathematics.encourages: A testable prediction about the chosen objective.
mathematics.doesNotGuarantee: Latent recovery claims are conditional: remove an assumption and the same observations can support a different latent explanation.

methodComparison.question: What does the assumption buy?
methodComparison.practicalImplication: Do not upgrade one kind of evidence into another.

evidence.claim: We observe activations and labels; latent causes are the hidden variables proposed to explain why those observations covary.
evidence.note: Course explanation. Use the research library's primary sources for paper-specific empirical claims.

failureModes.name: A score that answers the wrong question
failureModes.mechanism: The objective rewards a proxy while the paper describes a stronger scientific property.
failureModes.symptom: The headline metric rises, but seed stability, controls, or interventions disagree.
failureModes.diagnosticTest: Add a matched negative control and one counterfactual intervention.
failureModes.mitigation: Either narrow the claim or add the missing measurement.
failureModes.setconcaRelevance: Report both the intended benefit and the most plausible shortcut for every SetConCA loss term.

setconcaConnection.relevance: Observed variables versus latent causes determines how SetConCA should represent, group, or evaluate activation views.
setconcaConnection.whichPart: The choice of sparse code, view set, coordination loss, or evaluation.
setconcaConnection.whichDesignChoice: Match the pointwise SAE and SetConCA on model, activation site, width, sparsity budget, and compute.
setconcaConnection.whichFailure: Mistaking stronger agreement for recovery of the intended concept.
setconcaConnection.whichExperiment: Compare fidelity, feature stability, selectivity, and controlled interventions; report each result separately.
setconcaConnection.alternativeExplanation: An apparent gain may come from extra context, effective capacity, or easier positives rather than set coordination.

exercises.prompt: A paper claims that observed variables versus latent causes improved interpretability. What one result would you ask for before accepting that claim?
exercises.reveal: Ask for the smallest missing control: a matched baseline, several seeds, a negative control, or an intervention that tests the claimed mechanism.

masteryQuestions.prompt: Explain observed variables versus latent causes to a colleague, then name one thing the method cannot establish by itself.

nextLesson.bridge: Next: Correlation is not independence. The next idea reuses this lesson's representation and makes a new assumption explicit.

fields.label: Falsifiable claim
fields.prompt: Write the smallest claim this lesson supports.

fields.label: Control
fields.prompt: What control would rule out a shortcut?
